title.purpose: 11px uppercase, 0.05em tracking. In-panel section headers only — the one legitimate tracked-uppercase.

body.purpose: 13–15px system sans. Panel content, command rows, inputs.

label.purpose: 12px medium. Field labels, tool captions, text buttons.

annotation.purpose: The one loaded webfont. Hand-written free-text notes on the canvas, never in chrome.

shadows.name: island-lift
shadows.purpose: Canonical floating-chrome shadow — islands and the properties panel. Soft ambient + tight contact.

shadows.name: shadow-md
shadows.purpose: Token: default lift. Islands should migrate onto this from their hardcoded shadow.

shadows.name: shadow-lg
shadows.purpose: Token: lifts the command palette modal.

shadows.name: segment-lift
shadows.purpose: The raised active thumb of a segmented control.

motion.name: ease-out-expo
motion.purpose: Panel/modal entrances (command-palette slideIn). The one non-default easing.

motion.name: dur-border
motion.purpose: Input focus border shift; radial option transitions.

notes.breakpoints: None. Desktop-first, full-viewport single-canvas SPA; responsiveness is structural (floating slots), not breakpoint-driven.
notes.reducedMotion: GAP: no prefers-reduced-motion handling exists yet. fadeIn/slideIn/radial transitions should gain a reduced-motion fallback.
notes.fonts: The UI commits to the native OS fonts: system-ui for the sans role, the system monospace stack for the mono role. Only Caveat is web-loaded, for on-canvas annotations.

components.name: Icon Button (island)
components.description: The square 32×32 icon control used across every floating island; takes an indigo-soft active state.
components.html: <button class="ds-icon-btn" aria-label="Undo"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" width="16" height="16" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M3 7v6h6"/><path d="M21 17a9 9 0 0 0-9-9 9 9 0 0 0-6 2.3L3 13"/></svg></button>
components.css: .ds-icon-btn { display:flex; align-items:center; justify-content:center; width:32px; height:32px; border:none; border-radius:6px; background:transparent; color:#1a1a1a; cursor:pointer; transition:background-color .1s; } .ds-icon-btn:hover { background:rgba(0,0,0,0.06); } .ds-icon-btn:active { background:rgba(0,0,0,0.1); } .ds-icon-btn:focus-visible { outline:2px solid #4f46c9; outline-offset:2px; } .ds-icon-btn[aria-pressed="true"] { background:rgba(79,70,201,0.1); color:#4f46c9; }

components.name: Tool Button
components.description: Taller vertical tool control: icon stacked over a monospace shortcut badge.
components.css: .ds-tool-btn { display:flex; flex-direction:column; align-items:center; justify-content:center; gap:2px; width:40px; height:48px; border:none; border-radius:6px; background:transparent; color:#1a1a1a; cursor:pointer; transition:background-color .1s; } .ds-tool-btn:hover { background:rgba(0,0,0,0.06); } .ds-tool-btn:active { background:rgba(0,0,0,0.1); } .ds-tool-btn:focus-visible { outline:2px solid #4f46c9; outline-offset:2px; } .ds-tool-icon { display:flex; align-items:center; justify-content:center; height:20px; } .ds-tool-badge { font-size:11px; line-height:12px; color:#6b6b75; }

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The one filled-indigo CTA — the export action. Used once per surface.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display:inline-flex; align-items:center; height:32px; padding:0 12px; border:none; border-radius:6px; background:#4f46c9; color:#fff; font-family:var(--font-sans); font-size:12px; font-weight:500; cursor:pointer; transition:background-color .1s; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background:#443cb9; } .ds-btn-primary:active { background:#443cb9; opacity:.9; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline:2px solid #4f46c9; outline-offset:2px; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Standard field: hairline border, focus shifts border to Clinical Indigo — no glow.
components.css: .ds-input { width:100%; padding:6px 8px; border:1px solid #e6e6ec; border-radius:6px; font-family:var(--font-sans); font-size:13px; background:#fff; color:#1a1a1a; outline:none; transition:border-color .15s; } .ds-input::placeholder { color:#6b6b75; } .ds-input:focus { border-color:#4f46c9; }

components.name: Floating Island
components.description: The signature container: surface + hairline border + soft lift, floating over the canvas.
components.html: 100%
components.css: .ds-island { display:inline-flex; align-items:center; gap:4px; padding:6px; background:#fff; border:1px solid #e6e6ec; border-radius:10px; box-shadow:0 4px 16px rgba(0,0,0,0.08), 0 1px 3px rgba(0,0,0,0.06); } .ds-island-btn { display:flex; align-items:center; justify-content:center; width:32px; height:32px; border:none; border-radius:6px; background:transparent; color:#1a1a1a; font-size:16px; cursor:pointer; transition:background-color .1s; } .ds-island-btn:hover { background:rgba(0,0,0,0.06); } .ds-island-readout { font-size:12px; color:#6b6b75; min-width:48px; text-align:center; user-select:none; }

components.name: Shortcut Chip
components.description: Monospace keyboard-shortcut hint chip, as used in the command palette.
components.css: .ds-shortcut { font-family:var(--font-mono, monospace); font-size:11px; color:#6b6b75; background:#f7f7f9; border:1px solid #e6e6ec; border-radius:4px; padding:1px 5px; white-space:nowrap; }

components.name: Radial Menu Option
components.description: Signature circular add-relative button orbiting a symbol; hover fills indigo, active scales down.
components.css: .ds-radial-option { display:flex; align-items:center; justify-content:center; width:32px; height:32px; padding:2px; border:1px solid #e6e6ec; border-radius:50%; background:#fff; box-shadow:0 1px 4px rgba(0,0,0,0.1); color:#1a1a1a; font-family:var(--font-sans); font-size:8px; font-weight:600; line-height:1; letter-spacing:-0.2px; text-align:center; cursor:pointer; transition:background-color .15s, border-color .15s, transform .15s; } .ds-radial-option:hover { background:#4f46c9; border-color:#4f46c9; color:#fff; transform:scale(1.1); } .ds-radial-option:active { transform:scale(0.95); }

narrative.overview: Pedigree Canvas is a calm, well-lit surface where a clinician draws a family pedigree. The drawing IS the document; everything else is a tool laid at the edge of the table, within reach but never in the way. The canvas fills the entire viewport, and the chrome — a set of small floating islands — hovers over it rather than framing it. The personality is a quiet professional instrument: precise, calm, approachable. It descends visibly from Excalidraw but sheds the whiteboard's casualness where clinical seriousness is required. Warmth is carried by generous spacing, plain-clinical copy, and the option of a warmer canvas tint — never by decoration. Confidence is carried by restraint: familiar controls, tight radii, subtle state changes, and an accent used so sparingly that when it appears you know exactly what it means.
keyCharacteristics: Canvas-first: full-bleed drawing surface; chrome floats, never reflows the canvas.
On-demand UI: controls appear where and when needed (radial menu on a symbol, panel on selection).
One accent, used rarely: Clinical Indigo marks selection, primary action, and active state — nothing decorative.
Standards-locked canvas: symbols stay dark-on-light in every theme; fill is semantic (filled = affected).
Redundant encoding: colour is never the only channel — condition fills also carry a pattern.
Comfort, not modes: Light / Warm / Dim re-tint the surface; there is no dark mode.

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: Clinical Indigo appears on ≤10% of any screen and always means 'this is active / selected / the primary action.' If indigo is decorating something inert, it is wrong.

rules.name: The Semantic Fill Rule
rules.body: On the canvas, fill is meaning, not style. A filled symbol is affected; an open symbol is unaffected. Never invert to a dark theme. This is why there is no dark mode — only comfort themes that keep symbols dark-on-light.

rules.name: The Redundant-Colour Rule
rules.body: Any colour that encodes clinical meaning must be paired with a second channel — a fill pattern (diagonalLines, dots, crosshatch, stripes) or a label — so a colour-blind reader loses nothing.

rules.name: The Size-and-Weight Rule
rules.body: Hierarchy comes from size and weight within one sans, never a second UI typeface. The only additional family is Caveat, and only for on-canvas annotations.

rules.name: The Fixed-Scale Rule
rules.body: Product UI uses a fixed px scale, never clamp()/fluid type. A control that shrinks in a docked panel reads as broken, not responsive.

rules.name: The Lift-Means-Floating Rule
rules.body: A shadow is permission to overlap the canvas — nothing else. Flat surfaces stay flat; shadows never encode importance or decorate a resting card.

dos: Do keep the canvas full-bleed and let chrome float over it. The pedigree is the hero.
Do reserve Clinical Indigo (#4f46c9) for selection, the primary action, active state, and focus — the One Accent Rule.
Do pair every meaning-bearing colour with a second channel — a fill pattern or a label — so condition shading survives colour-blindness.
Do keep symbols dark-on-light in every theme; comfort themes re-tint warmth and luminance only.
Do use the radius (6/10/14px) and shadow tokens; give floating chrome the Island Lift shadow and nothing at rest.
Do build every interactive control with default / hover / focus / active / disabled states, and keep transitions short (100–150ms, state-only).
Do design directly for the native system-sans stack — there is no bundled UI webfont.
Do honor prefers-reduced-motion on the palette/menu entrances with a crossfade or instant fallback. (Not yet implemented — add it.)
donts: Don't build dated enterprise medical software: no dense gray toolbars, tiny fonts, or modal soup. Exhaust inline/progressive UI before a modal.
Don't go sterile or cold — warmth lives in spacing and plain-clinical copy.
Don't ship the generic SaaS dashboard: no card grids, no gradient hero-metrics, no purple-gradient buttons, no tracked-uppercase eyebrow above every section.
Don't go playful or gamified: no mascots, emoji, bounce/elastic easing, or candy palettes.
Don't introduce a dark mode or invert the canvas — it breaks the Semantic Fill Rule.
Don't add a second UI typeface or use clamp()/fluid type. Caveat is for on-canvas annotations only.
Don't use border-left/border-right > 1px as a coloured accent stripe, or background-clip:text gradient text.
Don't push muted text lighter than Ink Secondary (#6b6b75) for 'elegance' — it fails contrast on the near-white surfaces.
